what: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
text: Extracts k-gram patterns (subsequences length k) sequences group, computes standardized residuals independence model, optionally runs permutation chi-square test group differences.

title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
code: sequence_compare(   x,   group = NULL,   sub = 3:5,   min_freq = 5L,   test = c("permutation", "chisq", "none"),   iter = 1000L,   adjust = "fdr" )

title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
text: x netobject_group (grouped build_network), netobject (requires group), wide-format data.frame (requires group). group Character vector. Column name vector group labels. needed netobject_group. sub Integer vector. Pattern lengths analyze. Default: 3:5. min_freq Integer. Minimum frequency group pattern included. Default: 5. test Character. Inference method: one "permutation" (default), "chisq", "none". See Details. iter Integer. Permutation iterations. used test = "permutation". Default: 1000. adjust Character. P-value correction method (see p.adjust). Default: "fdr".

title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
text: object class "net_sequence_comparison" containing: patterns Tidy data.frame. Always present: pattern, length, freq_<group>, prop_<group>, resid_<group>. test = "permutation": effect_size, p_value. test = "chisq": statistic, p_value. groups Character vector group names. n_patterns Integer. Number patterns passing min_freq. params List sub, min_freq, test, iter, adjust.

title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
text: Standardized residuals always computed 2xG contingency table (pattern vs. everything else) using textbook formula (o - e) / sqrt(e * (1 - r/N) * (1 - c/N)). describe much group's count given pattern deviates expectation independence, scaled approximately N(0,1) null. optional test argument chooses inference method: "permutation" Shuffles group labels across sequences recomputes per-pattern statistic (row-wise Euclidean residual norm). Answers: "pattern's distribution associated group membership actor level?" Respects sequence unit analysis; can underpowered number sequences small. "chisq" Runs chisq.test 2xG table per pattern. Answers: "group streams generate pattern different rates?" Treats k-gram occurrence event; fast powerful even sequences, iid assumption makes optimistic sequences strongly autocorrelated. "none" Skip inference. residuals, frequencies, proportions returned. P-values adjusted across patterns (per-pattern) using method supported p.adjust. default "fdr" (Benjamini-Hochberg).

title: Compare Subsequence Patterns Between Groups — sequence_compare
code: seqs <- data.frame(   V1 = sample(LETTERS[1:4], 60, TRUE),   V2 = sample(LETTERS[1:4], 60, TRUE),   V3 = sample(LETTERS[1:4], 60, TRUE),   V4 = sample(LETTERS[1:4], 60, TRUE) ) grp <- rep(c("X", "Y"), 30) net <- build_network(seqs, method = "relative") res <- sequence_compare(net, group = grp, sub = 2:3, test = "chisq")

what: Sequence Plot (heatmap, index, or distribution) — sequence_plot
title: Sequence Plot (heatmap, index, or distribution) — sequence_plot
text: Single entry point three categorical-sequence visualisations. type = "heatmap" (default): dense carpet, rows reordered sort / dendrogram (single panel). type = "index": data layout, rows separated thin gaps (dendrogram). Supports grouping via group net_clustering, plus ncol x nrow facet grid. type = "distribution": dispatches distribution_plot.

title: Sequence Plot (heatmap, index, or distribution) — sequence_plot
code: sequence_plot(   x,   type = c("heatmap", "index", "distribution"),   sort = c("lcs", "frequency", "start", "end", "hamming", "osa", "lv", "dl", "qgram",     "cosine", "jaccard", "jw"),   tree = NULL,   group = NULL,   scale = c("proportion", "count"),   geom = c("area", "bar"),   na = TRUE,   normalize = FALSE,   row_gap = 0,   dendrogram_width = 1.2,   k = NULL,   k_color = "white",   k_line_width = 2.5,   state_colors = NULL,   na_color = "grey90",   cell_border = NA,   frame = FALSE,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   main = NULL,   show_n = TRUE,   time_label = "Time",   xlab = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   tick = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   nrow = NULL,   combined = TRUE,   legend = NULL,   legend_size = NULL,   legend_title = NULL,   legend_ncol = NULL,   legend_border = NA,   legend_bty = "n" )

title: Sequence Plot (heatmap, index, or distribution) — sequence_plot
text: x Wide-format sequence data. Accepts: data.frame / matrix Rows = sequences, columns = time points. netobject Extracts $data. net_clustering build_clusters. Uses $data, $assignments grouping, $distance dendrogram. netobject_group cluster_network build_network clustering. Extracts data assignments attr(, "clustering"). net_mmm build_mmm. Uses $models[[1]]$data $assignments. tna tna package. Decodes integer-encoded sequences. mcml build_mcml (built sequences). Produces multichannel plot: one panel per cluster plus macro Summary panel. type = "heatmap"/"index" draw carpet (channel's states solid, clusters faded wash); type = "distribution" draws stacked distribution (add normalize = TRUE TraMineR-style seqdplot time point sums 1). Returns ggplot object. type One "heatmap" (default), "index", "distribution". sort Row-ordering strategy heatmap / within-panel index. One "lcs" (default), "frequency", "start", "end", build_clusters distance ("hamming", "osa", "lv", "dl", "qgram", "cosine", "jaccard", "jw"). tree Optional hclust/dendrogram/agnes object supply row ordering (heatmap ; overrides sort). group Optional grouping vector (length nrow(x)) producing one facet per group. Index/distribution . Ignored heatmap. scale, geom, na Passed distribution_plot type = "distribution". normalize mcml + type = "distribution" . TRUE, time point normalised sum 1 within channel (TraMineR-style seqdplot composition); FALSE (default) stack shows prevalence capped NA band. row_gap Fraction row height used vertical gap sequences index plots. 0 (default) = dense like heatmap. Try 0.15 visible separators low row counts. dendrogram_width Width ratio dendrogram panel (heatmap). k Optional integer. supplied type = "heatmap", cuts dendrogram k clusters draws thin horizontal separators carpet. Ignored dendrogram (e.g. sort = "start") types. k_color Colour cluster separator lines. Default "white". k_line_width Line width cluster separators. Default 2.5. state_colors Vector colours, one per state. na_color Colour NA cells. cell_border Cell border colour. NA = . frame TRUE (default), draw box around panel. FALSE, box - axis ticks labels still appear. width, height Optional device dimensions inches. supplied, opens new graphics device via grDevices::dev.new(). knitr chunks use fig.width / fig.height chunk options instead. main Plot title. show_n Append "(n = N)" title. time_label, xlab X-axis label. xlab alias. y_label, ylab Y-axis label (distribution ). ylab alias. tick Show every Nth x-axis label. NULL = auto. ncol, nrow Facet grid dimensions (index + distribution). Ignored combined = FALSE. combined Index distribution types . TRUE (default), groups arranged one figure via graphics::layout(). FALSE, group drawn page (one full-size figure per group, legend). Single-group calls (G == 1) ignore argument. Heatmap always single-figure. legend Legend position: "bottom", "right", "none". Default varies type. legend_size Legend text size. NULL (default) auto-scales device width legend looks proportional 5 vs 12 figures (clamped [0.65, 1.2]). legend_title Optional legend title. legend_ncol Number legend columns. legend_border Swatch border colour. legend_bty "n" "o".

title: Self-Regulated Learning Strategy Frequencies — srl_strategies
text: Simulated frequency counts 9 self-regulated learning (SRL) strategies 250 university students. Strategies grouped three clusters: metacognitive (Planning, Monitoring, Evaluating), cognitive (Elaboration, Organization, Rehearsal), resource management (Help_Seeking, Time_Mgmt, Effort_Reg). Within-cluster correlations moderate (0.3–0.6), cross-cluster correlations weaker.

code: set.seed(1) dat <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(60), ncol = 3)) bg <- boot_glasso(dat, iter = 10, cs_iter = 5, centrality = "strength") summary(bg, type = "edges") #>       edge weight   ci_lower  ci_upper inclusion #> 1 V1 -- V2      0 -0.4571621 0.3104382       0.5 #> 2 V1 -- V3      0  0.0000000 0.5580798       0.8 #> 3 V2 -- V3      0 -0.4873334 0.0000000       0.6 # \donttest{ set.seed(42) mat <- matrix(rnorm(60), ncol = 4) colnames(mat) <- LETTERS[1:4] boot <- boot_glasso(as.data.frame(mat), iter = 20, cs_iter = 10,   centrality = "strength", seed = 42) summary(boot, type = "edges") #>     edge weight    ci_lower  ci_upper inclusion #> 1 A -- B      0 -0.11158199 0.2237170      0.35 #> 2 A -- C      0 -0.35185599 0.0000000      0.45 #> 3 B -- C      0 -0.30946821 0.2065199      0.25 #> 4 A -- D      0  0.00000000 0.3570903      0.50 #> 5 B -- D      0 -0.15048304 0.3105727      0.35 #> 6 C -- D      0 -0.03724162 0.1787669      0.15 # }

code: seqs <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","B","C","A"), V2 = c("B","C","A","B")) clusters <- list(G1 = c("A","B"), G2 = c("C")) cs <- build_mcml(seqs, clusters) summary(cs) #>   cluster size within_total between_out between_in #> 1      G1    2            1   0.3333333  1.0000000 #> 2      G2    1            0   1.0000000  0.3333333 # \donttest{ seqs <- data.frame(   T1 = c("A","B","A"), T2 = c("B","C","B"),   T3 = c("C","A","C"), T4 = c("A","B","A") ) clusters <- c("Alpha", "Beta", "Alpha") cs <- build_mcml(seqs, clusters, type = "raw") summary(cs) #>   cluster size within_total between_out between_in #> 1   Alpha    2            3           3          3 #> 2    Beta    1            0           3          3 # }

title: Summary Method for net_markov_order — summary.net_markov_order
code: # \donttest{ # First-order Markov data: test should select order 1 set.seed(1) states <- letters[1:4] tm <- matrix(runif(16), 4, 4, dimnames = list(states, states)) tm <- tm / rowSums(tm) seqs <- lapply(1:30, function(.) {   s <- character(50); s[1] <- sample(states, 1)   for (i in 2:50) s[i] <- sample(states, 1, prob = tm[s[i - 1], ])   s }) res <- markov_order_test(seqs, max_order = 3, n_perm = 300, seed = 1) res$optimal_order #> [1] 1 summary(res) #>   order    loglik      AIC      BIC  df        g2 p_permutation p_asymptotic #> 1     0 -2011.025 4028.050 4043.990  NA        NA            NA           NA #> 2     1 -1805.112 3640.224 3719.922   9 411.78019   0.003322259 4.193315e-83 #> 3     2 -1789.863 3703.726 4033.146  36  30.35171   0.803986711 7.337874e-01 #> 4     3 -1701.468 3808.936 4887.520 136 173.56575   0.099667774 1.633824e-02 #>   significant #> 1          NA #> 2        TRUE #> 3       FALSE #> 4       FALSE plot(res)  # }

title: Summary Method for net_permutation — summary.net_permutation
code: s1 <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","B","C"), V2 = c("B","C","A")) s2 <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","C","B"), V2 = c("C","B","A")) n1 <- build_network(s1, method = "relative") n2 <- build_network(s2, method = "relative") perm <- permutation(n1, n2, iter = 10) summary(perm) #>   from to weight_x weight_y diff effect_size   p_value   sig #> 1    A  B        1        0    1    1.333333 0.5454545 FALSE #> 2    A  C        0        1   -1   -1.267449 0.5454545 FALSE #> 3    B  A        0        1   -1   -1.360828 0.4545455 FALSE #> 4    B  C        1        0    1    1.360828 0.4545455 FALSE #> 5    C  A        1        0    1    1.237969 0.7272727 FALSE #> 6    C  B        0        1   -1   -1.184698 0.7272727 FALSE # \donttest{ set.seed(1) d1 <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","B","A"), V2 = c("B","C","B"),                  V3 = c("C","A","C")) d2 <- data.frame(V1 = c("C","A","C"), V2 = c("A","B","A"),                  V3 = c("B","C","B")) net1 <- build_network(d1, method = "relative") net2 <- build_network(d2, method = "relative") perm <- permutation(net1, net2, iter = 20, seed = 1) summary(perm) #>   from to weight_x weight_y diff effect_size p_value   sig #> 1    A  B        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 2    B  C        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 3    C  A        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE # }

code: s1 <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","B","A","C"), V2 = c("B","C","B","A"),   V3 = c("C","A","C","B"), grp = c("X","X","Y","Y")) s2 <- data.frame(V1 = c("C","A","C","B"), V2 = c("A","B","A","C"),   V3 = c("B","C","B","A"), grp = c("X","X","Y","Y")) nets1 <- build_network(s1, method = "relative", group = "grp") nets2 <- build_network(s2, method = "relative", group = "grp") perm  <- permutation(nets1, nets2, iter = 10) summary(perm) #>   group from to weight_x weight_y diff effect_size p_value   sig #> 1     X    A  B        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 2     X    B  C        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 3     X    C  A        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 4     Y    A  B        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 5     Y    B  C        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 6     Y    C  A        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE # \donttest{ set.seed(1) s1 <- data.frame(V1 = c("A","B","A","C"), V2 = c("B","C","B","A"),                  V3 = c("C","A","C","B"), grp = c("X","X","Y","Y")) s2 <- data.frame(V1 = c("C","A","C","B"), V2 = c("A","B","A","C"),                  V3 = c("B","C","B","A"), grp = c("X","X","Y","Y")) nets1 <- build_network(s1, method = "relative", group = "grp") nets2 <- build_network(s2, method = "relative", group = "grp") perm  <- permutation(nets1, nets2, iter = 20, seed = 1) summary(perm) #>   group from to weight_x weight_y diff effect_size p_value   sig #> 1     X    A  B        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 2     X    B  C        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 3     X    C  A        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 4     Y    A  B        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 5     Y    B  C        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE #> 6     Y    C  A        1        1    0           0       1 FALSE # }

what: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
text: Computes per-state branching entropy, stationary entropy, chain-level entropy rate Markov transition process. entropy rate Shannon-McMillan-Breiman per-step uncertainty trajectories stationary distribution; canonical information-theoretic summary transition matrix.

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
code: transition_entropy(x, base = 2, normalize = TRUE)

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
text: x netobject, cograph_network, tna object, row-stochastic numeric transition matrix, wide sequence data.frame (rows = actors, columns = time-steps; relative transition network built automatically). Group dispatch netobject_group. base Numeric. Logarithm base. 2 (default) bits, exp(1) nats, 10 hartleys. normalize Logical. TRUE (default), rows sum 1 normalised automatically (warning).

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
text: object class "net_transition_entropy" : row_entropy Named numeric vector, length \(n\). Per-state branching entropy \(H(P_{\cdot}) = -\sum_j P_{ij} \log P_{ij}\). row_entropy_norm Named numeric vector. row_entropy divided ceiling \(\log_b n\) (\([0, 1]\); zeros \(n = 1\)). stationary Named numeric vector. Stationary distribution \(\pi\). stationary_entropy Scalar. \(H(\pi) = -\sum_i \pi_i \log \pi_i\) - entropy \(\pi\) treated ..d. distribution. Upper bound entropy rate. stationary_entropy_norm Scalar. stationary_entropy divided ceiling \(\log_b n\). entropy_rate Scalar. \(h(P) = \sum_i \pi_i H(P_{\cdot})\) - Shannon-McMillan-Breiman entropy rate. entropy_rate_norm Scalar. entropy_rate divided ceiling \(\log_b n\). redundancy Scalar. \(H(\pi) - h(P)\), entropy deficit attributable serial dependence; zero ..d. chain (rows \(P\) equal \(\pi\)). redundancy_norm Scalar. relative redundancy \((H(\pi) - h(P)) / H(\pi)\) (fraction stationary entropy removed order-1 memory), redundancy divided \(\log_b n\); 0 \(H(\pi) = 0\). max_entropy Scalar. normalising ceiling \(\log_b n\). base Logarithm base used. states Character vector state names.

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
text: Convention \(0 \log 0 := 0\) applied, absorbing deterministic rows contribute zero per-row entropy. chain need irreducible; \(\pi\) computed eigendecomposition \(P^\top\) elsewhere package. non-ergodic chains returned \(\pi\) one stationary distribution among many - interpret help chain_structure. relation \(h(P) \leq H(\pi)\) holds equality iff successive states independent. deficit \(H(\pi) - h(P)\) reported redundancy - measure much memory chain order 1.

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
text: Cover, T.M. & Thomas, J.. (2006). Elements Information Theory, 2nd ed., chapter 4. Wiley. Shannon, C.E. (1948). mathematical theory communication. Bell System Technical Journal, 27, 379-423.

title: Transition Entropy of a Markov Chain — transition_entropy
code: # \donttest{ net <- build_network(as.data.frame(trajectories), method = "relative") te  <- transition_entropy(net) print(te) #> Transition Entropy (3 states, bits; ceiling = 1.585) #>  #>                           raw            normalised #>   Entropy rate    h(P)  = 1.247 bits    0.787 #>   Stationary    H(pi)  = 1.501 bits    0.947 #>   Redundancy   H(pi)-h = 0.255 bits    0.170 #>  #> Normalised: h(P) and H(pi) are raw / log_2(n_states) (0 = deterministic, 1 = uniform); #>   redundancy is the relative redundancy (H(pi) - h(P)) / H(pi), not raw / log_2(n_states). summary(te) #> Transition Entropy Summary (bits) #>  #> Per-state contribution to h(P): #>       state stationary row_entropy row_entropy_norm contribution #>     Average      0.443       1.354            0.855        0.600 #>      Active      0.372       1.040            0.656        0.387 #>  Disengaged      0.185       1.403            0.885        0.260 #>  contribution_pct #>              48.1 #>              31.0 #>              20.8 #>  #> Chain-level summary: #>               quantity   raw normalised #>      entropy_rate h(P) 1.247      0.787 #>       stationary H(pi) 1.501      0.947 #>  redundancy H(pi)-h(P) 0.255      0.170 #>       ceiling log_2(n) 1.585      1.000 #>  #> Normalised: row entropy, h(P) and H(pi) are raw / log_2(n_states), in [0, 1]; #>   redundancy is the relative redundancy (H(pi) - h(P)) / H(pi). plot(te)  # }

what: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
text: Non-parametric vertex bootstrap single observed network (Snijders & Borgatti 1999). Vertices resampled replacement weight matrix rebuilt original entries resampled vertex pairs; network-level statistics computed replicate give bootstrap distributions, standard errors, confidence intervals. Unlike bootstrap_network, resamples underlying cases (sequences rows) therefore requires raw data stored netobject, vertex bootstrap needs weight matrix. works netobject — including data-less ones build_mlvar constituents as_tna(mcml) elements — plain weight matrices. two procedures answer different questions: case bootstrap quantifies sampling--subjects uncertainty edge weights; vertex bootstrap quantifies structural uncertainty whole-network descriptives given one network observed.

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
code: vertex_bootstrap(   x,   iter = 1000L,   ci_level = 0.05,   ci_method = c("percentile", "basic"),   statistics = NULL,   statistic_fn = NULL,   directed = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
text: x netobject (build_network builder), cograph_network, square numeric weight matrix. iter Integer. Number bootstrap replicates (default 1000). ci_level Numeric. Significance level confidence intervals (default 0.05 95% CIs). ci_method Character. "percentile" (default) empirical quantile intervals, "basic" intervals reflected around observed value (Davison & Hinkley 1997, eq. 5.6). statistics Character vector selecting built-statistics (see Details). Default: applicable network's directedness. statistic_fn Optional named list functions, taking weight matrix returning single numeric value. Computed alongside built-ins. directed Logical NULL. Directedness network. NULL (default) reads x$directed available, otherwise falls back matrix symmetry. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed reproducibility.

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
text: object class "net_vertex_bootstrap" containing: summary Tidy data frame, one row per statistic: statistic, observed, boot_mean, boot_sd, bias, ci_lower, ci_upper. boot_stats iter x n_statistics matrix replicate values. observed Named vector observed statistics. iter, ci_level, ci_method, directed, n_nodes Configuration.

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
text: replicate draws n vertex indices replacement sets W_b[, j] = W[idx_i, idx_j]. original vertex drawn two different positions, -diagonal cell structural self-pair; following Snijders & Borgatti, cells filled weight randomly chosen pair distinct original vertices. Diagonal entries carry original self-weight resampled vertex (W[idx_i, idx_i]) — self-loops meaningful transition networks never altered. undirected networks substitution applied symmetrically replicates stay symmetric. Built-statistics (computed -diagonal part weight matrix): density Proportion non-zero -diagonal cells. mean_weight Mean non-zero -diagonal weights. centralization Freeman-type strength centralization: sum(max(s) - s) / ((n - 1) * max(s)) s total node strength absolute weights. 0 nodes equal strength, approaching 1 star. reciprocity Directed networks . Weighted reciprocity sum(pmin(|W|, |t(W)|)) / sum(|W|) -diagonal cells: proportion total weight reciprocated.

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
text: Snijders, T. . B., & Borgatti, S. P. (1999). Non-parametric standard errors tests network statistics. Connections, 22(2), 161-170. Davison, . C., & Hinkley, D. V. (1997). Bootstrap Methods Application. Cambridge University Press.

title: Vertex Bootstrap for Network-Level Statistics — vertex_bootstrap
code: seqs <- data.frame(   T1 = c("plan", "code", "debug", "plan", "test", "code"),   T2 = c("code", "debug", "code", "plan", "code", "test"),   T3 = c("debug", "code", "plan", "code", "debug", "plan"),   T4 = c("test", "plan", "test", "debug", "plan", "code") ) net <- build_network(seqs, method = "relative") vb <- vertex_bootstrap(net, iter = 100, seed = 1) vb$summary #>        statistic  observed boot_mean    boot_sd        bias   ci_lower #> 1        density 0.8333333 0.8433333 0.13415153  0.01000000 0.58333333 #> 2    mean_weight 0.3800000 0.3734656 0.05149123 -0.00653441 0.27899554 #> 3 centralization 0.3589744 0.2255932 0.12632830 -0.13338118 0.04511327 #> 4    reciprocity 0.5939850 0.5726307 0.16772881 -0.02135424 0.13107527 #>    ci_upper #> 1 1.0000000 #> 2 0.4725536 #> 3 0.4635149 #> 4 0.8545707 # \donttest{ plot(vb)  # }

what: Compare Network-Level Statistics of Two Networks — vertex_compare
title: Compare Network-Level Statistics of Two Networks — vertex_compare
text: Snijders & Borgatti (1999) two-network test: network's statistics get vertex-bootstrap standard errors, difference tested $$z = (\hat{\theta}_x - \hat{\theta}_y) /   \sqrt{SE_x^2 + SE_y^2}$$ standard normal reference. comparison vertex bootstrap originally proposed : deciding whether two observed networks differ density, centralization, reciprocity, whole-network descriptive.

title: Compare Network-Level Statistics of Two Networks — vertex_compare

title: Compare Network-Level Statistics of Two Networks — vertex_compare
text: x, y two networks: netobjects, cograph_networks, square weight matrices, precomputed net_vertex_bootstrap objects (iter, statistics, statistic_fn, directed, seed ignored argument). two sides must cover exactly statistics; mismatch (e.g., directed network's reciprocity undirected one, precomputed objects built different statistics selections) error, never silent subset. iter Integer. Number bootstrap replicates (default 1000). ci_level Numeric. Significance level confidence intervals (default 0.05 95% CIs). statistics Character vector selecting built-statistics (see Details). Default: applicable network's directedness. statistic_fn Optional named list functions, taking weight matrix returning single numeric value. Computed alongside built-ins. directed Logical NULL. Directedness network. NULL (default) reads x$directed available, otherwise falls back matrix symmetry. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed reproducibility. labels Character vector length 2 naming networks output (default c("x", "y")).

title: Compare Network-Level Statistics of Two Networks — vertex_compare

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
text: Computes networks one-hot (binary indicator) data using temporal windowing. Supports transition (directed), co-occurrence (undirected), network types.

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
code: wtna(   data,   method = c("transition", "cooccurrence", "both"),   type = c("frequency", "relative"),   codes = NULL,   window_size = 3L,   mode = c("non-overlapping", "overlapping"),   actor = NULL )

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
text: data Data frame one-hot encoded columns (0/1 binary). method Character. Network type: "transition" (directed), "cooccurrence" (undirected), "" (returns list two networks). Default: "transition". type Character. Output type: "frequency" (raw counts) "relative" (row-normalized probabilities). Default: "frequency". Note type = "relative" applied method = "cooccurrence" produces asymmetric matrix (conditional co-occurrence given row state), symmetric undirected weight matrix — use type = "frequency" symmetric co-occurrence counts required. codes Character vector NULL. Names one-hot columns use. NULL, auto-detects binary columns. Default: NULL. window_size Integer (>= 1). Number consecutive rows aggregate per window. Default: 3 (windowed pairwise -window counting). Set window_size = 1 ordinary consecutive (t -> t+1) transitions windowing. mode Character. Window mode: "non-overlapping" (fixed, separate windows) "overlapping" (rolling, step = 1). Default: "non-overlapping". actor Character NULL. Name actor/ID column per-group computation. NULL, treats rows one group. Default: NULL.

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
text: method = "transition" "cooccurrence": netobject (see build_network). method = "": wtna_mixed object elements $transition $cooccurrence, netobject.

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
text: Transitions: Uses crossprod(X[-n,], X[-1,]) count often state active time t state j time t+1. Co-occurrence: Uses crossprod(X) count states simultaneously active row. Windowing: window_size > 1, rows aggregated windows computing networks. Non-overlapping windows fixed, separate blocks; overlapping windows roll forward one row time. Within window, active indicator (1) row makes state active window. Per-actor: actor specified, networks computed per group summed.

title: Window-based Transition Network Analysis — wtna
code: oh <- matrix(c(1,0,0, 0,1,0, 0,0,1, 1,0,0), nrow = 4, byrow = TRUE,              dimnames = list(NULL, c("A","B","C"))) w <- wtna(oh)  # \donttest{ # Simple one-hot data df <- data.frame(   A = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1),   B = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 0),   C = c(0, 0, 1, 0, 0) )  # Transition network net <- wtna(df) print(net) #> Network (method: wtna_transition) [directed] #>   Weights: [1.000, 2.000]  |  mean: 1.333 #>  #>   Weight matrix: #>     A B C #>   A 2 2 0 #>   B 1 1 0 #>   C 1 1 0  #>  #>   Initial probabilities: #>   A             1.000  ████████████████████████████████████████ #>   B             0.000   #>   C             0.000    # Both networks nets <- wtna(df, method = "both") print(nets$transition) #> Network (method: wtna_transition) [directed] #>   Weights: [1.000, 2.000]  |  mean: 1.333 #>  #>   Weight matrix: #>     A B C #>   A 2 2 0 #>   B 1 1 0 #>   C 1 1 0  #>  #>   Initial probabilities: #>   A             1.000  ████████████████████████████████████████ #>   B             0.000   #>   C             0.000   print(nets$cooccurrence) #> Network (method: wtna_cooccurrence) [undirected] #>   Weights: [1.000, 5.000]  |  mean: 2.333 #>  #>   Weight matrix: #>     A B C #>   A 5 3 2 #>   B 3 2 1 #>   C 2 1 1   # With windowing net <- wtna(df, window_size = 2, mode = "non-overlapping") # }

title: Nestimate 0.7.3
text: certainty() — analytic Bayesian counterpart bootstrap_network() transition networks. Models state’s outgoing transitions Dirichlet-Multinomial process (Jeffreys prior) returns posterior mean, sd, credible interval stability decision per edge closed form (resampling). Returns exact net_bootstrap object layout carries class c("net_certainty", "net_bootstrap"), drop-: every net_bootstrap method works . Completes assessment trio certainty / stability (bootstrap_network) / reliability (reliability).

title: Nestimate 0.7.3
text: sequence_plot() gains multichannel view mcml objects built sequences. sequence_plot(fit) draws one carpet panel per cluster channel plus macro Summary panel — channel’s states solid, clusters faded wash, finished cells white, rows aligned macro sequence. sequence_plot(fit, type = "distribution") stacks prevalence (states + faded clusters + explicit NA band, 100%), normalize = TRUE gives TraMineR-style seqdplot time point sums 1. ggplot-based dependency-free; returns ggplot object. bayes_compare() results now 100% compatible permutation() format: object carries class c("net_bayes", "net_permutation") net_permutation slots (diff_sig, p_values, effect_size, iter, alpha, paired, adjust), summary superset summary.net_permutation (, , weight_x, weight_y, diff, effect_size, p_value, sig plus Bayesian extras count_x, count_y, ci_lower, ci_upper, ci_width, pd). bayes_compare() result now drop-wherever net_permutation consumed.

title: Nestimate 0.7.2
text: bayes_compare() — Bayesian Dirichlet-Multinomial comparison two transition networks, complement permutation(). Models source state’s outgoing transitions Dirichlet-Multinomial process (Jeffreys prior) returns, per edge, posterior mean difference, credible interval, probability direction (pd) two-sided p-equivalent. Adds print/summary/plot methods netobject_group dispatch (-pairwise matched). Method source: Johnston & Jendoubi (2026), Delivery Mode Reshapes Resource Engagement: Bayesian Differential Network Analysis, TNA Workshop 2026.

title: Nestimate 0.7.1
text: as_networks() — promote build_mcml_pc() result netobject_group (psychometric-network counterpart as_tna()). Singleton clusters within-network dropped warning; existing netobject_group passes unchanged.

title: Nestimate 0.7.1
text: Vignettes articles now call package verbs directly instead hand-assembled base-R subsetting rituals: markov_order_test() reads sequences straight fitted network (markov_order_test(net)); HYPA anomaly tables use summary(hypa, order_by = "ratio"); higher-order pathways use pathways(hon, top = ); grouped-clustering inspection uses cluster_diagnostics().

title: Nestimate 0.7.0
text: build_mcml_pc() — MCML aggregation psychometric networks (cor / pcor / EBICglasso). Five aggregation methods explicitly different statuses: "average" (descriptive block-mean; works without raw data), "composite" (cluster scores re-estimated chosen estimator — genuine cluster-level network), "loadings" (composites weighted mean within-cluster connection strength — Nestimate’s weighting, EGA reimplementation), "rv" (Escoufier’s RV matrix correlation blocks), "canonical" (first canonical correlation — upper bound composite methods). Within-cluster networks re-estimated default (within = "reestimate") since pcor submatrix subsystem’s pcor network. Item diagnostics $loadings: signed loadings (reverse-keyed items detected via leading eigenvector within-block matrix flipped composites), cross-cluster strengths, misfit flag item connected another cluster (warned). Composites tolerate missing data (row-wise renormalized weighted means); Composite item weights selectable via weighting — ten built-schemes spanning three views cluster: scale scored ("equal", "item_total"), network’s view ("strength", "eigen", "closeness", "betweenness", "expected_influence", "specificity" — misfit margin weighting, zeroing items belong much another cluster), latent-variable view ("pca", "factor"); plus fully custom weighting via named numeric vector function(W_block, data_block, nodes). aggregation = "loadings" alias composite + strength. "factor" weighting exposes extraction method via fa_method: "ml" (factanal), "paf" (iterated principal axis), "minres" (ULS), "cfa" (one-factor lavaan model; cor_method = "polychoric" categorical DWLS factor model) — operating cor_method-consistent correlation structure. Reverse-keyed handling works every sign-carrying scheme (item-total correlations computed eigen-sign-pre-oriented columns reversed member contaminate small clusters). cor_method = "polychoric" (via lavaan) supports ordinal items; id_col drops identifier columns convert_sequence_format(format = "frequency") actor-profiles feed function directly (within-person co-occurrence view event data). Returns class mcml_pc (macro + within netobjects, undirected) print/summary/plot; composite/loadings macro full netobject, bootstrap_network(), vertex_bootstrap(), vertex_compare() apply directly. cograph::plot_mcml() (>= 2.3.8) renders two-layer undirected MCML view. Experimental: API formulas may change. loading_stability() — case-bootstrap stability build_mcml_pc() composite weights (percentile CIs, sign-flip rates), print forest-style plot.

title: Nestimate 0.6.5
text: vertex_bootstrap() — Snijders & Borgatti (1999) vertex bootstrap network-level statistics (density, mean weight, strength centralization, weighted reciprocity, plus custom statistic_fn). Needs weight matrix, works data-less netobjects (build_mlvar() constituents, as_tna(mcml) elements, plain matrices) bootstrap_network() run. Returns tidy one-row-per-statistic net_vertex_bootstrap print/summary/plot. Self-loops preserved (diagonal carries resampled vertex’s self-weight); undirected replicates stay symmetric. vertex_compare() — Snijders & Borgatti two-network test vertex bootstrap originally proposed : z-tests normal-approximation CIs differences network-level statistics two networks (netobjects, matrices, precomputed net_vertex_bootstrap objects). Tidy net_vertex_comparison result print/summary/plot (forest plot differences). bootstrap_network() vertex_bootstrap() gain ci_method = c("percentile", "basic"): basic intervals (Davison & Hinkley 1997, eq. 5.6) reflect percentile bounds around observed estimate, correcting first-order bootstrap bias. Default remains "percentile".

title: Nestimate 0.6.4
text: build_mcml() (sequence edge-list paths) now records effective directedness $meta$directed: FALSE type = "cooccurrence", whose weights symmetrized, instead echoing directed argument unchanged. Renderers auto-detect directedness (e.g., cograph::plot_mcml() directed = NULL) now draw co-occurrence MCML objects undirected networks automatically.

title: Nestimate 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-31

title: Nestimate 0.6.0
text: magnitude_difference() compares frequency (FTNA) probability (TNA) views transition network quantifies per-edge discrepancy common scale, five metrics, four scalings, two polar plot() portraits (stacked circular). Full persistent homology Vietoris-Rips filtration (persistent_homology(), build_simplicial(type = "vr")) plus diagram tools bottleneck_distance() persistence_landscape(). Network comparison: compare_model() (netobject_group dispatch), summary.netobject(), plot.net_comparison(), rename_models() relabelling grouped network objects.

title: Nestimate 0.6.0
text: pkgdown reference index now lists every exported function; magnitude_difference(), casedrop_reliability(), build_hypergraph(), hypergraph_measures(), cluster_data() previously absent.

title: Nestimate 0.6.0
text: Removed Remotes: field; cograph tna available CRAN, non-CRAN source pin needed.

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: Followed codex_docs/audit_clustering codex_docs/audit_mcml recommendations across two modules. Eleven thirteen findings addressed; two deferred pending design decisions numeric semantics (directed = FALSE raw-data MCML, MMM first-non-NA initial state).

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: cluster_network() now forwards distance-clustering arguments (na_syms, weighted, lambda, seed, q, p, covariates) build_clusters() instead silently passing build_network(). split runs caller ... — netobject build_args continue flow build_network() step, protecting attention-method (atna) network history re-routed weighted Hamming. (audit_clustering #1) .auto_detect_clusters() (used build_mcml() cluster_summary()) now requires node_groups carry node identifier column shaped data.frame, named atomic vector keyed node label. Previously, bare cluster-data.frame read positionally — silently mis-assigning nodes whenever node_groups rows different order x$nodes. (audit_mcml #1) build_clusters() now rejects -missing input early clear message instead failing indirectly downstream pam/hclust. (audit_clustering #4)

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: compare_mmm(return_fits = FALSE) — TRUE, fitted net_mmm models attached attr(result, "fits") keyed k, users can pick chosen model without re-running EM. Default behaviour unchanged. (audit_clustering #6)

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: build_clusters() validation messages now name offending argument ("'k' must least 2 (got k = 1)") rather dumping failing predicate. Top-level type checks switched named-condition stopifnot() reason. (audit_clustering #2)

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: summary.mcml() roxygen corrected — claiming printing side effect doesn’t exist. (audit_mcml #5) build_mcml() clusters = "<col>" mode now documents narrow contract: assigns row’s group label endpoints, makes sense within-group edge lists. (audit_mcml #2) build_mcml() method parameter doc now steers raw sequence / event-log inputs "sum", since function counts observed transitions. methods weighted edge lists pre-existing matrices. (audit_mcml #4) as_tna.mcml() “Excluded Clusters” section corrected — drop emits warning() (claimed silent) fires relative method (claimed unconditional). (audit_mcml #6) build_clusters() na_syms doc adds explicit “Missing-value distance rule” subsection: NA becomes comparable sentinel state, pairwise deletion. (audit_clustering #3) build_mmm() adds “Initial states” section explaining first-column-verbatim init build_mmm honor build_clusters-style na_syms — actual NA cells become NA inits. (audit_clustering #5, doc-path)

title: Nestimate 0.5.1
text: +12 new tests pinning corrected contracts documented edge cases (misordered node_groups alignment, label propagation state_distribution(), as_tna.mcml() drop-warning fixture, MMM first-column NA behaviour, four-way cluster_network() arg-routing contract). Full sweep: 1628 / 1628 pass, 0 fail.

title: Nestimate 0.5.0
text: .extract_edges_from_matrix() longer drops diagonal. Netobjects built via .wrap_netobject() (therefore everything build_network(), build_mcml(), bootstrap_network(), build_mmm(), wtna(), as_tna()) now $edges containing every non-zero matrix entry, including self-loops. Previously $weights $edges silently inconsistent matrix non-zero diagonal, causing downstream consumers (e.g. cograph::centrality() MCML macro) -count node degree 2.

title: Nestimate 0.5.0
text: plot_state_frequencies() — native S3 generic state-frequency plots across netobject, netobject_group, mcml, htna. Defaults marimekko (mosaic) layout column widths reflect per-group totals segment heights reflect within-group state proportions; also supports colored-bars style per-group faceted marimekko. Uses package Okabe-Ito palette throughout. plot_mosaic() — exported low-level marimekko primitive built geom_rect() cumulative-width / cumulative-height geometry. Reusable tidy data.frame(group, state, weight) input.

title: Nestimate 0.4.4
text: passage_time() markov_stability() now raise explicit error naming dead state transition-matrix row sums zero, instead silently propagating NaN eigen/solve. Zero rows mean chain ergodic; mean first passage times undefined. Shared helper .mpt_normalize_rows() factored entry points. .prepare_association_input() longer hard-rejects non-square numeric matrices. association methods (glasso, pcor, cor) netobject’s $data slot numeric matrix (data.frame). downstream caller row-subsetted $data re-invoked estimator (centrality_stability(), bootstrap_network(), reliability()) silently producing NULL centralities caught tryCatch, surfaced “centrality measures zero variance” warning -NaN correlations. matrix branch now recognises non-square input raw observation data recursively re-enters data-frame branch. Square symmetric matrices (pre-computed correlation / covariance) still go symmetric-matrix path symmetry check intact.

title: Nestimate 0.4.4
text: build_network() gains state_cols metadata_cols parameters (default NULL). Explicit overrides state-vs-metadata column classifier, previously used “values--nodes” heuristic silently misclassifies metadata columns whose values coincide node labels (e.g. condition column levels "","B","C" nodes "","B","C"). Validation: error overlap two vectors, error column names present input data. Forwarded group = ... recursive dispatch per-group calls honour override.

title: Nestimate 0.4.4
text: plot.net_link_prediction() plot.mcml() removed. Nestimate computation engine — visualization user’s concern. Previously methods called cograph:: directly, violating stated dependency invariant (Nestimate -> cograph direction forbidden). Users call cograph::splot(net) cograph::plot_mcml(fit) directly.

title: Nestimate 0.4.4
text: wtna() @param type now flags type = "relative" combined method = "cooccurrence" produces asymmetric matrix (conditional co-occurrence given row state), symmetric undirected weight matrix. Use type = "frequency" symmetric counts required.

title: Nestimate 0.4.4
text: New numerical-equivalence tests (gated NESTIMATE_EQUIV_TESTS=true): test-equiv-permutation.R (vs. stats::p.adjust + hand-coded base-R permutation loop), test-equiv-mlvar.R (vs. mlVAR::mlVAR machine precision), test-equiv-association-rules.R (vs. arules::apriori), test-equiv-link-prediction.R (vs. clean-room matrix algebra + igraph::similarity), test-equiv-centrality-stability.R (vs. bootnet::corStability). Total ~162k per-value comparisons; within machine precision except centrality-stability uses documented drop-grid tolerance bootnet uses igraph path-based centrality Nestimate uses Floyd-Warshall. New HON-family equivalence tests local_testing_and_equivalence/ validating HON, HONEM, HYPA, MOGen, hypergraph pathpy 2.2.0 (via reticulate), BiasedUrn, RSpectra, HyperG. shipped R-package tests/ directory; added .Rbuildignore. Branch-matrix coverage added four many-mode APIs (wtna, bootstrap_network, build_clusters, sequence_plot) — systematic cross-product tests combinations mode parameters catch regressions one branch silently diverges.

title: Nestimate 0.4.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-20

title: Nestimate 0.4.3
text: Tarball now ships build/vignette.rds (vignette index). Previous 0.4.2 build used R CMD build ---build-vignettes, preserved pre-built inst/doc/*.html stripped index — CRAN flagged “VignetteBuilder field prebuilt vignette index.” test-gimme.R now skip_on_cran(). GIMME tests fit lavaan SEM per subject took ~50s locally (2-3× Windows), pushing total check time 11 min win-devel. Full test suite still runs CI local dev.

title: Nestimate 0.4.2
text: Full ---cran --run-donttest audit pass. Purged stale .Rcheck/ Meta/ build artifacts working tree; added explicit ^Nestimate\.Rcheck$ ^\.\.Rcheck$ entries .Rbuildignore belt--suspenders repeat-submission contamination.

title: Nestimate 0.4.1
text: Pre-built vignettes included inst/doc/ required CRAN. Fixed 301-redirect URLs README. Added skip_on_cran() slow test block keep check time 10 minutes.

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: build_mlvar() — multilevel VAR networks ESM/EMA panel data. Estimates temporal (directed), contemporaneous (undirected), -subjects (undirected) networks matching mlVAR::mlVAR() machine precision. build_mmm() / compare_mmm() — mixture Markov models via EM, BIC/AIC/ICL model selection optional covariate regression. cooccurrence() — standalone co-occurrence network builder supporting 6 input formats 8 similarity methods. sequence_compare() — k-gram pattern comparison across groups optional permutation testing. sequence_plot() / distribution_plot() — base-R sequence index state distribution plots clustering integration. build_simplicial(), persistent_homology(), q_analysis() — topological analysis networks via simplicial complexes. nct() — Network Comparison Test matching NetworkComparisonTest::NCT() machine precision. build_gimme() — group iterative mean estimation idiographic networks via lavaan. passage_time(), markov_stability() — Markov chain passage times stability analysis. predict_links() / evaluate_links() — link prediction 6 structural similarity methods. association_rules() — Apriori association rule mining sequences binary matrices. predictability() — node predictability glasso/pcor/cor networks. build_hon(), build_honem(), build_hypa(), build_mogen() — higher-order network methods (HON, HONEM, HYPA, MOGen) now cograph_network-compatible.

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: human_long, ai_long — canonical long-format human–AI pair programming interaction sequences (10,796 turns, 429 sessions). chatgpt_srl — ChatGPT-generated SRL scale scores psychological network analysis. trajectories — 138-student engagement trajectory matrix (15 timepoints, 3 states).

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: build_clusters(), network_reliability(), permutation(), prepare() replace earlier internal names consistency build_* naming convention. mgm estimator added (method = "mgm") mixed continuous + categorical data via nodewise lasso, matching mgm::mgm() machine precision.

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: build_mmm() longer crashes platforms parallel::detectCores() returns NA (macOS ARM64 CRAN check failure). gimme convergence filter now correctly handles typed NA variants (NA_character_, NA_real_, etc.). NaN values numeric metadata aggregation (-NA sessions) normalized NA_real_. HYPA p-values corrected; hypa_score column renamed p_value.

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: .data pronoun added globalVariables(). base::.rowSums() / base::.colSums() replaced rowSums() / colSums(). dev.new() guarded interactive() — side effects knitr CI. Equivalence test files excluded built tarball.

title: Nestimate 0.4.0
text: .call(rbind, ...) replaced data.table::rbindlist() mcml.R sequence_compare.R.

title: Nestimate 0.3.4
text: HYPA: Renamed hypa_score column p_value clarity. Added $, $, $n_over, $n_under fields net_hypa objects. Scores now pre-sorted anomalous paths first. HYPA: summary.net_hypa() now shows /-represented paths separately configurable n parameter. pathways.netobject(): New S3 method extract higher-order pathways directly netobject (builds HON HYPA internally). path_counts(): Now handles NAs trajectories stripping k-gram counting.

title: Nestimate 0.2.15
text: Preparing publication

title: Nestimate 0.2.0
text: Reduced hard dependencies 6 4 Imports (ggplot2, glasso, data.table, cluster). Removed igraph Imports — centrality_stability() boot_glasso() now accept centrality_fn parameter external centrality computation. Removed tna Imports — moved Suggests (used input class detection). Implemented graphical_var() scratch using coordinate descent lasso + graphical lasso EBIC model selection, eliminating graphicalVAR dependency. Dropped ml_graphical_var() — users use mlvar() multilevel VAR. Removed cograph plot wrappers — plot.netobject(), plot.net_bootstrap(), plot.net_permutation(), plot.net_hon(), plot.net_hypa() as_cograph() removed. Users call cograph plotting functions directly netobjects. Added attention estimator decay-weighted transition networks. Increased test coverage 84.5% 96.1% (2780 tests). Passes R CMD check 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.

title: Nestimate 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Split Saqrlab v0.3.0. Core estimation via build_network() 8 built-estimators. Bootstrap inference (bootstrap_network()), permutation testing (permutation()), EBICglasso bootstrap (boot_glasso()). Higher-order networks: HON, HONEM, HYPA, MOGen. GIMME, MCML, multilevel VAR, graphical VAR. Temporal network analysis velocity TNA. Dual-class c("netobject", "cograph_network") output cograph compatibility.
